name: defuddle
tagline: Wraps the Defuddle CLI to pull clean markdown from web pages instead of WebFetch, cutting token usage on articles and docs.
verdict: Small, tightly scoped skill with clear positive and negative triggers (skips .md URLs), concrete commands, and an install fallback for the missing-tool case. Only gaps: it globally installs the unpinned defuddle npm package from the default registry (low risk, legitimate small tool, no red flags) and carries no version or changelog signal.

highlights.summary: `npm install -g defuddle` installs an unpinned, unversioned global package from the default registry.
No version field, changelog, or dated revision notes anywhere in frontmatter or body.
